figures.caption: Fig. 2: Overall pipeline of the proposed DeferredSeg framework based on MedSAM.

Fig. A.5: DeferredSeg interactive expert-annotation interface. The page shows (top) model prediction and routing results, (middle) per-expert canvases for interactive corrections, and (bottom) the fused segmentation and ground-truth comparison.
Table C.8: Effect of varying coherence (λ1) and balance (λ2) weights on System / Expert /Model performance (%).
Table 3: Comparison of different methods across multiple datasets, organs, and evaluation metric (%). Row-wise best values are bold.

Fig. 1: Overview of DeferredSeg. A deferral predictor produces a pixel-wise deferral map that triages image regions between a human expert and the base segmentation model, enabling spatially adaptive Human–AI collaboration for reliable medical segmentation.
Fig. C.6: Complete set of heatmaps for all branches and metrics.

Fig. 4: Expert scalability. System DSC and Jaccard reach their highest values at J=1, drop at J=2, and partially recover for J≥3, remaining within a narrow band for J=3–5. System Sensitivity varies slightly across settings but attains its maximum at J=5.
Table 6: Analysis of each loss component through ablation. These components are mutually complementary and indispensable.

Fig. 3: (a) Qualitative results under the 1-expert (top) and 3-expert (bottom) settings. (b) Deferral maps where brighter regions indicate higher deferral probability. (c) Heatmap of System Jaccard over coherence (λ1) and balance (λ2), with optimal λ1=1, λ2=5.

Table D.10: Performance on PROMISE12 under different numbers of experts (%). Single expert yields peak overlap (DSC/Jaccard), whereas multiple experts raise Sensitivity. (mean ± std; row-wise best in bold, second best in italics)
Table 5: CENet baseline vs. CENet with L2D under 3 experts (mean ± std, %). Row-wise best values are bold.
